steps.title: Identify what the blank needs
steps.content: After "However," the blank must start a new independent clause finishing "______ the most recognition for voice acting." That requires a subject and a verb. The subject must agree in number with its antecedent in the passage.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'those get': "those" is plural and has no clear antecedent — the passage names one person, Keith David, not a group of things. ✗
  • Choice B — 'both get': "both" is plural and implies exactly two referents, but no two comparable things have been named. ✗
  • Choice C — 'he gets': "he" is singular and matches the antecedent Keith David; the sentence reads smoothly: "However, he gets the most recognition for voice acting." ✓
  • Choice D — 'they all get': "they all" is plural with no plural antecedent; it also contradicts the idea that one person (David) is singled out for voice acting. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
